questions.question: How many columns of bits need to be flipped in this puzzle?
hints: There are 3 columns: B (bottom), M (middle), T (top)

questions.question: How is the path split so that blue balls can flip the first two columns?
questions.expected_answer: Use one bit at the bottom to split the path
hints: A bit at the bottom sends balls to different columns on alternating balls

questions.question: How is the third column (T) flipped?
questions.expected_answer: With a red ball triggered by the second blue ball
hints: After the second blue ball triggers the right lever, the red ball flips T column

questions.question: Describe the sequence of balls flipping each column
questions.expected_answer: Blue ball 1: flips B column; Blue ball 2: flips M column and triggers red; Red ball: flips T column
hints: Second blue flips middle column and triggers red lever
Red ball then flips top column
